senses.definition: the skater's overall posture, demeanor, and expression while performing in figure skating

examples.x: Her graceful attitude on the ice captivated the audience.
His charismatic attitude drew applause from the crowd at the end of his performance.
She skated with a fierce attitude, commanding attention from the judges.
His relaxed attitude helped him execute the jumps with precision.
She adopted a serene attitude, reflecting the music's tranquil melody.

senses.definition: a classical ballet position where the dancer stands on one leg with the other leg extended behind, often with arms in various graceful positions

examples.x: The ballerina held a perfect arabesque, her body forming a beautiful line as she balanced effortlessly on one leg.
During the performance, the dancers showcased their strength and flexibility with elegant arabesques, adding a touch of sophistication to the routine.
She practiced her arabesques diligently, striving to achieve the ideal balance and alignment in each position.
With fluid movements, the lead dancer transitioned seamlessly from a pirouette into a graceful arabesque, showcasing her technical skill and artistry.

senses.definition: a spin in figure skating where the skater lifts one leg into the air behind them while spinning on the ice

examples.x: Tim's camel spin ended with a graceful exit, impressing the audience.
Emily struggled to maintain balance in her camel spin at first.
Lucy's camel spin was the highlight of her performance at the competition.
Sarah executed a flawless camel spin during her figure skating routine.
The coach provided feedback to Jessica on how to extend her leg higher in the camel spin.

senses.definition: (figure skating) a rotational movement where the skater spins backward on one foot while maintaining momentum

examples.x: Sarah executed a flawless backspin during her figure skating routine.
Emily struggled to maintain balance during her backspin.
Mark's backspin speed impressed the judges at the competition.
The audience applauded loudly at the skater's stunning backspin finish.

senses.definition: a figure skating move where the skater holds one leg extended upward behind the body while spinning on the ice

examples.x: Jessica's coach praised her improved Biellmann spin in practice.
Kevin admired the gracefulness of the Biellmann spin performed by his favorite skater.
Sam's Biellmann spin drew cheers from the crowd at the competition.
The judges awarded high scores for the skater's elegant Biellmann spin.
The Biellmann spin requires strong core muscles and flexibility.

senses.definition: a spin in figure skating where the skater leans backward and arches their body while spinning on one foot

examples.x: Sam focused on perfecting the entry and exit of his layback spin.
Alex worked on increasing the speed and rotation of his layback spin for competition.
Sarah's layback spin impressed the judges with its flexibility and extension.
Mark practiced his layback spin to improve his balance and control.
Emily executed a flawless layback spin during her figure skating routine.

senses.definition: a spinning element in figure skating where the skater rotates while maintaining a seated position on the ice

examples.x: Jessica's sit spin variation added creativity to her routine.
The synchronized team synchronized their sit spins perfectly during their routine.
David struggled to achieve the required speed in his sit spin rotations.
Alex's sit spin demonstrated excellent control and grace on the ice.
The skater's sit spin technique showed remarkable improvement since last season.

senses.definition: a spinning element in figure skating where the skater rotates in a crouched position with one leg extended horizontally and the other tucked in

examples.x: Emily performed a shotgun spin with precision and speed during her routine.
The skater's shotgun spin showcased their flexibility and control.
During rehearsal, Sam focused on maintaining balance in his shotgun spin.
Jessica's shotgun spin entry impressed the judges with its speed.
The shotgun spin requires strength and agility to execute effectively.

senses.definition: a spinning element in figure skating where the skater rotates in an upright position with both legs parallel and the body straight

examples.x: The upright spin requires a strong core and precise positioning for stability.
Sarah practiced her upright spin to improve her spin speed.
Anna's upright spin ended with a graceful flourish, impressing the judges.
The synchronized team synchronized their upright spins flawlessly.
The coach provided tips to enhance the skater's upright spin technique.

senses.definition: a spinning element in figure skating where the skater spins with an arched back, head dropped backward, and one leg pulled above the head from behind

examples.x: His fast rotation in the pearl spin impressed the judges.
She struggled to maintain her speed in the pearl spin.
His pearl spin entry was smooth and graceful.
She worked tirelessly to improve the height of her pearl spin.
She focused on her arm position to enhance the presentation of her pearl spin.

senses.definition: a spinning element in figure skating where the skater jumps into the air before rotating rapidly with one leg extended

examples.x: Her flying spin drew gasps from the audience with its elegance and precision.
He added a flying spin to the end of his program for an exciting finish.
She incorporates variations of the flying spin to add flair to her performances.
His flying spin sequence wowed spectators with its speed and height.
The flying spin is one of her signature moves in competitions.

senses.definition: a spinning element in figure skating where the skater rotates with both legs extended horizontally and parallel to the ice

examples.x: He executed a quick transition into the pancake spin from a spiral sequence.
Her pancake spin variation showcased her flexibility and control.
He finished his program with a dramatic pancake spin, earning applause from the crowd.
He struggled to maintain speed during his pancake spin.
His pancake spin showcased his excellent control and balance.

senses.definition: a challenging maneuver in figure skating where the male partner spins while holding the female partner in a low position with one hand, creating a spiral shape

examples.x: She executed a flawless death spiral during her routine.
The pair's chemistry was evident during the intricate death spiral maneuver.
He stumbled slightly on the exit of the death spiral but regained composure quickly.
The pair's death spiral was the highlight of their performance.
She narrowly avoided a fall during the death spiral sequence.

senses.definition: a graceful move in figure skating where the skater extends one leg outward while leaning backward, creating a fan-like shape with the body

examples.x: She executed a beautiful fan spiral across the ice.
His fan spiral showcased both grace and flexibility.
She added a unique twist to her routine with a fan spiral.
She transitioned seamlessly from a spin into a fan spiral.
His fan spiral demonstrated exquisite control and extension.

senses.definition: a move in figure skating where the male skater holds the female skater's hand and leans backward while she spirals around him with one leg lifted and tilted inward

examples.x: Her partner supported her beautifully as she extended into the backward inside death spiral.
They received high scores for their seamless backward inside death spiral.
She practiced tirelessly to perfect the backward inside death spiral.
He lifted her effortlessly into the backward inside death spiral, wowing the audience.
The judges praised their unique variation of the backward inside death spiral.

senses.definition: a move where the skater glides on one foot with the other leg extended backward, forming a horizontal line

examples.x: She executed a beautiful Charlotte spiral across the ice.
His Charlotte spiral showcased his flexibility and extension.
She transitioned seamlessly from a spin into a Charlotte spiral.
He incorporated a Charlotte spiral into his program's choreography.
She finished her routine with a breathtaking Charlotte spiral.

senses.definition: a figure skating jump where the skater kicks both legs behind in the air and lands on a back outside edge

examples.x: He nailed the timing on his butterfly jump.
She's working on increasing the height of her butterfly jump.
His butterfly jump showcased his athleticism and artistry.
She incorporated a series of butterfly jumps into her program.
His butterfly jump soared gracefully across the ice.

senses.definition: a jump in figure skating where the skater rotates in the air before landing on the same edge

examples.x: His Rippon jump earned him high scores from the judges.
She executed a flawless Rippon jump in her routine.
His Rippon jump was the highlight of his performance.
Her Rippon jump showcased her flexibility and control.
She's been practicing her Rippon jump to perfect the technique.

senses.definition: a jump in figure skating where the skater spins in the air, completing one or more rotations before landing

examples.x: She struggled with the timing of her rotational jumps during practice.
Her coach emphasized the importance of tight rotational jumps in her movements.
He focused on improving the height of his rotational jumps to impress the judges.
His program included a combination of both single and double rotational jumps.
She gained confidence in her rotational jumps after mastering the proper technique.

senses.definition: a figure skating maneuver where one skater lifts and holds their partner above the ice in various positions

examples.x: They executed a flawless carry lift in their pairs routine.
The carry lift added elegance to their performance.
She struggled to maintain balance during the carry lift.
Their coach emphasized proper technique for the carry lift.
She focused on her extension and posture during the carry lift.

senses.definition: a performance where two skaters execute identical movements simultaneously, facing each other

examples.x: Mirror skating involves mimicking the movements of your partner on the ice.
They practiced mirror skating to perfect their synchronicity for the competition.
Mirror skating requires precise timing and coordination between partners.
The duo impressed the judges with their flawless mirror skating routine.
Their mirror skating performance was a highlight of the exhibition.

senses.definition: an act of rotating one's body 360 degrees in the backward direction

examples.x: The gymnast amazed the crowd with a perfect backflip off the balance beam.
He practiced for weeks to learn how to land a backflip safely.
The daring skateboarder attempted a backflip during the competition.
She celebrated her win by performing a backflip on the field.
The instructor demonstrated the correct technique for a backflip in the gym class.

senses.definition: (figure skating) a position with one leg forward and the other backward
a dramatic and daring move in figure skating where the skater drops into a deep split while spinning rapidly

examples.x: She nailed the death drop in her routine.
Mastering the death drop takes skill.
He practiced the death drop tirelessly.
Her transition into the death drop was flawless.
Adding the death drop heightened the excitement.

senses.definition: a move in figure skating where the skater leans out horizontally from one leg

examples.x: The skater executed a flawless cantilever, showcasing their strength and flexibility.
After weeks of practice, she finally achieved the perfect cantilever.
She incorporated a unique twist into her cantilever, adding complexity to her routine.
His cantilever extended elegantly across the ice, captivating the audience.
The judges awarded high scores for the skater's innovative use of the cantilever.

senses.definition: a fundamental skating move in figure skating, involving pushing off with one foot while the other remains ahead

examples.x: Strong legs aid in powerful open strokes.
Beginners often struggle with open stroke balance.
Mastering the open stroke is crucial for figure skaters.
Skaters strive for perfect open stroke execution.
Proper open stroke technique is fundamental.

senses.definition: (figure skating) a move where the skater changes direction by skating on different edges of the blades in a smooth, curved motion

examples.x: He struggled with the timing of his bracket turn in practice.
He smoothly transitioned into a bracket turn from his spiral sequence.
She incorporated a series of bracket turns into her step sequence.
She needs to improve the extension in her bracket turn.
She's been practicing her bracket turns to improve her footwork.

senses.definition: (figure skating) a performance where skaters showcase jumps, spins, and footwork to music

examples.x: She's competing in the free skating event tomorrow.
He loves the freedom of expression in free skating.
Free skating allows skaters to showcase their creativity and technical skills.
Skaters train tirelessly for the precision required in free skating.
She's known for her artistry and grace in free skating.

senses.definition: (figure skating) a directional shift from a forward to a backward edge, or vice versa

examples.x: His elegant Choctaw turn impressed the judges at the competition.
She focused on maintaining speed throughout her Choctaw turn.
His coach emphasized the importance of edge control in the Choctaw turn.

senses.definition: a maneuver where the skater rocks onto the front or back of their blade to change direction smoothly

examples.x: She struggled with the rocker turn initially but eventually perfected it.
The rocker turn is often incorporated into complex skating sequences.
Skaters must maintain control and balance throughout the rocker turn.
Mastering the rocker turn is essential for advancing in figure skating.
She stumbled slightly on the rocker turn but quickly regained her balance.

senses.definition: a figure skating move where the skater glides on one foot while the other foot is extended backward and slightly turned out

examples.x: The skater executed a flawless Ina Bauer as part of her choreography.
She transitioned seamlessly from a spiral into an elegant Ina Bauer.
His Ina Bauer showcased both flexibility and control on the ice.
The coach emphasized the importance of extending the free leg fully in the Ina Bauer position.

senses.definition: a figure skating jump where the skater takes off from the back inside edge of one foot, rotates in the air, and lands on the same foot

examples.x: She executed a flawless toe loop jump during her performance.
Learning the toe loop jump requires precise timing and technique.
His toe loop jump was the highlight of his routine.
He practiced his toe loop jump repeatedly until he mastered it.
She struggled with her toe loop jump but eventually landed it perfectly.
